Title: Payment Form - Field Labels and Placeholders
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- CyberSource payment form visible on {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; locate CyberSource payment section
[ACT] Observe all payment field labels without interacting
[ASSERT] Each field has a visible label: 'Card Number', 'Cardholder Name', 'Expiry Date', 'CVV'
[ASSERT] Placeholder text is present and descriptive in each field
[ASSERT] Required fields are marked with asterisk (*) or equivalent required indicator
Assertions:
[DOM] All four payment fields have visible labels
[DOM] Placeholder text present and readable in all fields
[DOM] Required field indicator (* or 'required') present on mandatory fields
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No rendering errors for payment section labels
Failure_Signals: Any field missing a label; placeholder text absent or generic (e.g., '...' only); required indicator absent on mandatory fields

Title: Payment Form - Keyboard Tab Order
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- CyberSource payment form visible on {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
- Keyboard-only navigation mode (no mouse)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; locate CyberSource payment section
[ACT] Place focus on 'Card Number' field using keyboard (Tab from previous element)
[KEY] Press Tab to advance through payment fields
[ASSERT] Focus moves: Card Number → Cardholder Name → Expiry Date → CVV in logical order
[KEY] Verify Tab moves into iframe fields correctly (focus enters iframe)
[KEY] Verify Tab exits iframe fields correctly (focus leaves iframe to next storefront element)
[ASSERT] No tab trap encountered — Tab always advances focus
Assertions:
[STATE] Tab order follows logical sequence: Card Number → Cardholder Name → Expiry Date → CVV
[STATE] Focus enters and exits iframe fields without becoming trapped
[DOM] Focus indicator visible on each field when focused via keyboard
Cross_Layer_Checks: [CONSOLE] No JS errors from iframe focus handling
Failure_Signals: Tab order illogical (e.g., skips fields or jumps out of order); tab trap occurs inside iframe (cannot escape with Tab); focus indicator not visible on focused field

Title: Payment Form - Load Time Under 2 Seconds
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- Browser DevTools Performance or Network tab available
- Clear browser cache before test
Steps:
[NAV] Clear cache; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[ACT] Start timing from page navigation until CyberSource payment form is fully interactive
[WAIT] Observe payment section loading — iframe(s) must fully initialize
[ASSERT] CyberSource payment form is interactive within 2 seconds of page load completing
[ASSERT] All iframe fields accept input without additional waiting
[ACT] Open DevTools Network tab; review iframe resource load times
Assertions:
[STATE] CyberSource payment form fields interactive within 2 seconds of cart page load
[DOM] All payment iframe(s) initialized — fields accept clicks and input
[NETWORK] Iframe resources load without excessive blocking time
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] No failed resource loads for cybersource.com iframe scripts
[CONSOLE] No timeout errors for iframe initialization
Failure_Signals: Payment form remains in loading state beyond 2 seconds; iframe fails to initialize requiring page refresh; form fields unresponsive after iframes load

Title: Double-Click Place Order Prevention
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- CyberSource payment form on {{FRONT_URL}}/cart fully filled with valid test card
- DevTools Network tab open and recording
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; fill CyberSource payment form completely with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.expiry)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cvv)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cardholder)'
[ACT] Double-click 'Place Order' button as rapidly as possible
[WAIT] Wait for all network requests to settle
[ASSERT] Only ONE createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation request is sent — not two
[ASSERT] 'Place Order' button becomes disabled/inactive after the first click
[ASSERT] Only one order is created — check order confirmation shows single order number
[NAV] Navigate to account order history and verify only one new order appears
Assertions:
[STATE] 'Place Order' button disabled after first click — subsequent clicks have no effect
[STATE] Only one order created — no duplicate in account order history
[NETWORK] Only one createOrderFromCart mutation request in DevTools Network
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation called exactly once
[ADMIN] Single new order in admin — no duplicate orders with same cart contents and timestamp
Failure_Signals: Button remains active after first click (not disabled); two or more createOrderFromCart mutations sent; duplicate order appears in account history or admin
Cleanup: Cancel order(s) via admin — ensure duplicate orders also cancelled if created

Title: Declined Card - Error Message Displayed
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- Payment processor configured with a test card number known to produce a decline response
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[KEY] For CyberSource: payment form is on cart page. For other processors: click 'Place Order' first to reach {{FRONT_URL}}/checkout/payment
[ACT] Fill payment form using a known-decline test card (obtain from processor test docs or .env)
[ACT] Fill valid expiry date, CVV, and cardholder name
[ACT] Click 'Place Order'
[WAIT] Wait for processor decline response
[ASSERT] Clear error message displayed — e.g., 'Your card was declined' or equivalent
[ASSERT] Payment form fields are NOT cleared (user can correct and retry)
[ASSERT] User remains on payment step — not navigated away
[NAV] Navigate to account order history
[ASSERT] No new order appears in order history
Assertions:
[DOM] Error message displayed with text indicating card decline (not a generic system error)
[STATE] Payment form fields retain entered values after decline — not cleared
[NAV] User remains on payment step after decline
[STATE] No order created — order history unchanged
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation errors[] contains decline-related error OR mutation is not called if processor blocks before order creation
[ADMIN] No new order in admin for this session
Failure_Signals: No error message shown after decline; generic 'something went wrong' message without explanation; form cleared requiring full re-entry; order created despite decline (BL-PAY-005 violation)

Title: Declined Card - Retry with Different Card Succeeds
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- Payment in declined state from PAY-DECLINE-001 (decline error visible, no page reload performed)
Test_Data: FRONT_URL={{FRONT_URL}}, USER_EMAIL={{USER_EMAIL}}, USER_PASSWORD={{USER_PASSWORD}}, TEST_CARD_NUMBER={{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}}, TEST_CARD_EXP={{TEST_CARD_EXP}}, TEST_CARD_CVV={{TEST_CARD_CVV}}
Steps:
[KEY] Begin from declined state — error message visible, user on payment step, no page reload
[ACT] Clear 'Card Number' field and enter {{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}} (valid test card)
[ACT] Update 'CVV' field with {{TEST_CARD_CVV}}; update 'Expiry Date' if needed
[ACT] Click 'Place Order' again
[WAIT] Wait for payment processing response
[ASSERT] Payment succeeds with the new card — no error message
[NAV] User navigated to order confirmation page
[ASSERT] Order confirmation shows order number
[NAV] Check account order history
[ASSERT] Exactly one new order — no duplicate from the first declined attempt
Assertions:
[NAV] Order confirmation reached after retry with valid card
[DOM] Order confirmation displays order number
[STATE] Only one order created — no duplicate from failed first attempt
[MATH] Order total matches original cart total
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation errors[] is empty on successful retry
[ADMIN] Single new order in admin — no duplicate entry for this session
Failure_Signals: Retry attempt triggers page reload losing cart state; second valid card also declined; two orders created (one from declined attempt — BL-PAY-005 violation); form not accepting input after decline

Title: Declined Card - Network Timeout Recovery
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- Payment form fully filled with valid test card
- Browser DevTools Network tab available for throttling/blocking
Test_Data: FRONT_URL={{FRONT_URL}}, USER_EMAIL={{USER_EMAIL}}, USER_PASSWORD={{USER_PASSWORD}}, TEST_CARD_NUMBER={{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}}, TEST_CARD_EXP={{TEST_CARD_EXP}}, TEST_CARD_CVV={{TEST_CARD_CVV}}
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to payment step with valid card details filled in
[ACT] In DevTools Network tab: block or simulate timeout on the payment API endpoint
[ACT] Click 'Place Order'
[WAIT] Wait for timeout error response
[ASSERT] Error message displayed: 'Payment timed out. Please try again.' or equivalent — no internal server error details exposed
[ASSERT] Form remains usable — fields accessible for retry
[ACT] In DevTools: restore network (unblock payment API)
[ACT] Click 'Place Order' again
[WAIT] Wait for payment response
[ASSERT] Retry succeeds — order confirmation reached
[NAV] Check account order history and admin for duplicate orders
Assertions:
[DOM] Timeout error message visible — user-friendly text without server internals exposed
[STATE] Payment form fields remain accessible after timeout error
[NAV] Successful retry after network restored reaches order confirmation
[STATE] Only one order in history and admin — no duplicate from timeout
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] Only one createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation per user action — no automatic retries that could cause duplicates
[CONSOLE] No internal server error details leaked to console or UI
[ADMIN] Single order in admin after successful retry
Failure_Signals: Internal server error message exposed to user; form inaccessible after timeout requiring page refresh; duplicate order created from timeout + retry scenario; timeout produces no user feedback
Cleanup: Cancel order via admin; ensure no duplicate orders

Title: Declined Card - Double-Click Prevention After Error
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item
- Payment just returned a decline error — user is on payment step with error message visible
- DevTools Network tab open and recording
Steps:
[KEY] Begin from declined payment state — error visible, no page reload, form fields intact
[ACT] Without clearing the form: rapidly double-click or triple-click 'Place Order'
[WAIT] Wait for all network activity to settle
[ASSERT] Only ONE payment request is sent per action — not multiple rapid-fire requests
[ASSERT] 'Place Order' button is disabled immediately after first click
[ASSERT] Button re-enables after payment response received (allowing deliberate retry)
[ASSERT] No additional orders created beyond what the single payment request would produce
Assertions:
[STATE] 'Place Order' button disabled immediately after first click even following a decline
[NETWORK] Only one payment/order request per click sequence — no duplicate requests
[STATE] Button re-enables after response (enabling intentional retry)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] Only one createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation per click sequence visible in DevTools
[ADMIN] No duplicate orders created from rapid clicking after decline
Failure_Signals: Button not disabled after click following decline — multiple payment requests sent; multiple duplicate auth requests in Network tab; button requires page reload to re-enable after decline
Cleanup: none — verify no spurious orders; cancel any created via admin

Title: Declined Card - Cart and Checkout State Preserved
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item with specific quantity
- All checkout steps completed through to payment (address, shipping method selected)
- Payment submitted using a known-decline test card
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; proceed through checkout — enter shipping address, select shipping method, reach payment step
[KEY] Note the cart contents, item quantities, shipping address, and shipping method before payment
[ACT] Submit payment using known-decline test card; wait for decline response
[ASSERT] Error message displayed for decline
[ACT] Observe cart — navigate to cart page or review checkout summary
[ASSERT] Cart still contains all original items with correct quantities
[NAV] Review shipping address in checkout
[ASSERT] Shipping address is preserved — not reset to default or cleared
[NAV] Review shipping method selection
[ASSERT] Shipping method selection preserved — not reset
Assertions:
[STATE] Cart items and quantities unchanged after payment decline
[STATE] Shipping address preserved after payment decline — user need not re-enter
[STATE] Shipping method selection preserved after payment decline

[ADMIN] No new order in admin for this cart session

Title: Guest Checkout - CyberSource Payment Completes Without Account
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- User is NOT logged in (anonymous / guest session)
- Guest checkout enabled in store settings
- Cart has at least one item added as guest
- CyberSource payment processor configured and active
Steps:
[NAV] Open a fresh incognito/private browser window; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] User is not logged in — no user name or account icon in header
[ACT] Add at least one product to cart
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] Wait for CyberSource payment form to render in the guest cart view
[ACT] Enter guest email GUEST_EMAIL in the guest checkout email field (if prompted)
[ACT] Fill shipping address fields with valid test address data
[ACT] Fill 'Card Number' iframe field with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number)'
[ACT] Fill 'Cardholder Name' with 'Guest User'; fill 'Expiry Date' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.expiry)'; fill 'CVV' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cvv)'
[ACT] Click 'Place Order'
[WAIT] Wait for order processing response
Assertions:
[DOM] CyberSource payment form renders correctly for unauthenticated / guest session — no authentication wall
[NAV] After successful payment guest user is navigated to order confirmation page
[DOM] Order confirmation page shows order number and ordered items
[STATE] No account registration required to complete payment
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation response has no errors[] entries
[NETWORK] Payment token (not raw card number) sent in request payload for guest session
[ADMIN] Order appears in admin Orders list with guest email and correct payment method
Failure_Signals: CyberSource payment form fails to render for guest users; guest is forced to create an account before payment form appears; order confirmation not reached without registration; raw card data in network payload for guest session
Cleanup: none — guest order; no cleanup required unless test order removal is standard practice
References: ECL-14.1; BL-PAY-003; BL-CHK-005; STALE-BY-DESIGN: CyberSource Microform is NOT offered to guest sessions (user-confirmed by design 2026-06-05, REG-2026-06-05-1752 dismissed BUG_041_001) — expectation wrong; rewrite to assert guest-available payment methods; do NOT file 'form missing for guest' as a bug

Title: Guest Checkout - Payment Validation Same as Authenticated User
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- User is NOT logged in (anonymous / guest session)
- Guest checkout enabled in store settings
- Cart has at least one item added as guest
- CyberSource payment form visible on {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
Steps:
[NAV] Open a fresh incognito/private browser window; add product to cart; navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[ASSERT] User is not logged in — guest context confirmed
[WAIT] Wait for CyberSource payment form to render
[ACT] Leave 'Card Number' field empty; click 'Place Order'
[ASSERT] Inline validation error appears on 'Card Number' field — same as authenticated user behavior
[ACT] Fill 'Card Number' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_INVALID.number)' (Luhn-failing); click 'Place Order'
[ASSERT] 'Invalid card number' or equivalent error displayed
[ACT] Fill 'Card Number' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number)'; fill 'Expiry Date' with a past date (e.g., '01/2020'); click 'Place Order'
[ASSERT] Expired date validation error displayed on 'Expiry Date' field
[ACT] Fill valid card details: '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.expiry)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cvv)'; leave 'Cardholder Name' empty; click 'Place Order'
[ASSERT] Validation error for missing cardholder name (or form submits — document behavior)
Assertions:
[DOM] Each validation error appears at field level for guest users — not a single generic error
[STATE] Form does not submit with any invalid guest payment input
[NAV] Guest user remains on cart page after each validation failure
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] No createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation fired for any invalid payment attempt
[CONSOLE] No JS errors from CyberSource iframe during guest validation flows
Failure_Signals: Validation skipped for guest users while enforced for authenticated users; generic top-level error replaces field-level errors in guest context; form submits with invalid card in guest session
References: ECL-14.1; ECL-9.1; BL-PAY-003; BL-CHK-005; STALE-BY-DESIGN: CyberSource Microform is NOT offered to guest sessions (user-confirmed by design 2026-06-05) — validation-parity premise invalid for CyberSource; rewrite against guest-available payment methods

Title: Cross-Layer - Order Total at Checkout Matches Gateway Charge Amount
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Cart has at least one item with a known price (record the expected total)
- CyberSource payment processor active
- Browser DevTools Network tab open and recording
- Admin access available
Test_Data: FRONT_URL={{FRONT_URL}}, BACK_URL={{BACK_URL}}, USER_EMAIL={{USER_EMAIL}}, USER_PASSWORD={{USER_PASSWORD}}, ADMIN_EMAIL={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, TEST_CARD_NUMBER=@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number), TEST_CARD_EXP=@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.expiry), TEST_CARD_CVV=@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cvv)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart; record the displayed order total (subtotal + shipping + taxes)
[ASSERT] Displayed total matches expected value based on cart contents
[ACT] Fill CyberSource payment form: '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cardholder)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.expiry)', '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cvv)'
[ACT] In DevTools Network tab start recording; click 'Place Order'
[WAIT] Wait for order confirmation page
[ACT] In DevTools Network tab locate the CyberSource tokenization / payment authorization request; inspect the 'amount' or 'orderAmount' field in the request payload
[ASSERT] Amount in payment gateway request equals the total displayed in the cart
[NAV] On order confirmation page: record the total displayed
[ASSERT] Confirmation page total matches the cart total recorded before payment
[NAV] Log in to {{BACK_URL}} admin; locate the order; open order details
[ASSERT] Order total in admin matches the cart and confirmation totals
Assertions:
[MATH] Amount in CyberSource payment request payload equals displayed cart total (to 2 decimal places)
[MATH] Order confirmation total equals cart total — no rounding or truncation
[MATH] Admin order total equals confirmation total — consistent across all three surfaces
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] CyberSource payment request 'amount' field value matches storefront displayed total
[API] createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation totalPrice equals displayed cart total
[ADMIN] Order total in admin details matches storefront and network payload amounts
Failure_Signals: Amount in network request differs from displayed total (overcharge or undercharge — BL-CHK-006 violation); confirmation total differs from cart total; admin order total differs from payment amount; rounding inconsistency causes 1-cent discrepancy

Title: Payment Method Switch - Form Resets and Processor Unregisters When Switching
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered user
- Fresh cart with at least one item (IMPORTANT: start from a fresh cart for each switch direction — cart-resolver appends payment rows on every switch; orphaned rows from prior switches cause false test state)
- CyberSource and Skyflow payment processors both enabled on store
- CyberSource payment form visible on {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
Test_Data: FRONT_URL={{FRONT_URL}}, USER_EMAIL={{USER_EMAIL}}, USER_PASSWORD={{USER_PASSWORD}}, TEST_CARD_NUMBER=@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number), TEST_CARD_EXP=@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.expiry), TEST_CARD_CVV=@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cvv), sky_card=@td(SKYFLOW_MC.number), sky_exp=@td(SKYFLOW_MC.expiry), sky_cvv=@td(SKYFLOW_MC.cvv)
Steps:
--- Switch direction 1: CyberSource → Skyflow ---
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart with fresh cart; confirm both CyberSource and Skyflow options visible
[ACT] Select 'Bank card (CyberSource)' payment method
[WAIT] Wait for CyberSource Flex Microform iframe fields to render
[ACT] Fill 'Card Number' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.number)'; fill 'Cardholder Name' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cardholder)'; fill 'Expiry Date' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.expiry)'; fill 'CVV' with '@td(CYBERSOURCE_VISA.cvv)'
[ASSERT] All four CyberSource fields contain entered data
[ACT] Switch to 'Bank card (Skyflow)'
[WAIT] Wait for Skyflow inline form to render
[ASSERT] Skyflow iframe fields are visible; CyberSource form fields are hidden or removed from DOM
[ASSERT] 'Place order' button is DISABLED (CyberSource onUnmounted called setCardDataInvalid; Skyflow not yet filled)
[ASSERT] No stale CyberSource card data persisted in DOM
--- Switch direction 2: Skyflow → CyberSource (fresh cart) ---
[PRE:RESET_CART]
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart with fresh cart
[ACT] Select 'Bank card (Skyflow)'
[WAIT] Wait for Skyflow inline form to render on /cart
[ACT] Fill '@td(SKYFLOW_MC.number)' in Skyflow card field; fill '@td(SKYFLOW_MC.expiry)' and '@td(SKYFLOW_MC.cvv)'
[ACT] Switch to 'Bank card (CyberSource)'
[WAIT] Wait for CyberSource form to render
[ASSERT] CyberSource Flex Microform iframe fields are visible; Skyflow form absent from DOM
[ASSERT] CyberSource fields are empty — no Skyflow card data leaked
[ASSERT] 'Place order' button is DISABLED (Skyflow onUnmounted called setCardDataInvalid; CyberSource not yet filled)
Assertions:
[DOM] After CS→Skyflow switch: Skyflow form renders; CyberSource form absent; no stale CS data
[STATE] After CS→Skyflow switch: 'Place order' button disabled until Skyflow fields filled (onUnmounted cleanup fired)
[DOM] After Skyflow→CS switch: CyberSource form renders; Skyflow form absent; no stale Skyflow data
[STATE] After Skyflow→CS switch: 'Place order' button disabled until CS fields filled (onUnmounted cleanup fired)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during either switch direction — no 'processor already registered' errors
[NETWORK] No payment tokenization request fired when switching payment methods
[CONSOLE] No TypeError on stale processor reference after unmount
Failure_Signals: Stale card data persists in DOM after switching away; 'Place order' button stays enabled after switch (onUnmounted setCardDataInvalid not called — processor cleanup regression); JS error on switch; wrong payment form shown for selected method
References: ECL-7.1; BL-PAY-004; Moved from 040 to 041 (cross-cutting: covers CS↔Skyflow switch; PAY-SKY-011 covers Skyflow↔CS specific teardown; PAY-AN-011 covers AN→manual guard); Synced: VCST-5009 PR#2308+PR#23 (2026-06-04)

Title: Manual Payment - Purchase Order Placed with Pending Payment Status
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
- Logged in as registered B2B user (organization member with PO payment permission)
- Cart has at least one item
- 'Purchase Order' or 'Wire Transfer' (manual/offline) payment method enabled in store settings
- Admin access available to verify order status
Test_Data: FRONT_URL={{FRONT_URL}}, BACK_URL={{BACK_URL}}, USER_EMAIL={{B2B_USER_EMAIL}}, USER_PASSWORD={{B2B_USER_PASSWORD}}, ADMIN_EMAIL={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, ADMIN_PASSWORD={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, PO_NUMBER=PO-TEST-{{TIMESTAMP}}
Steps:
[NAV] Log in as {{B2B_USER_EMAIL}} / {{B2B_USER_PASSWORD}}; add at least one item to cart
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/cart
[WAIT] Wait for payment methods to render
[ACT] Select payment method displayed as 'Manual' (label may say 'Manual' or contain 'Purchase Order' — the storefront displays it as 'Manual' with an 'Enter a purchase order number' textbox)
[ASSERT] PO reference number textbox appears — fill with PO_NUMBER
[ASSERT] No card number, CVV, or expiry date fields are shown for manual payment
[ACT] Click 'Place Order'
[WAIT] Wait for order confirmation response
[ASSERT] Order confirmation page displayed with order number
[ASSERT] Payment status on confirmation indicates 'Pending Payment' or equivalent (not 'Paid')
[NAV] Log in to {{BACK_URL}} admin with {{ADMIN_EMAIL}} / {{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}; locate the order in Orders list
[ASSERT] Order in admin shows payment status as 'Pending' or 'Awaiting Payment'
[ASSERT] Order status allows fulfilment workflow to proceed (not blocked at payment)
Assertions:
[DOM] Manual payment option selectable without triggering card input fields
[NAV] Order confirmation page reached after selecting manual payment and clicking 'Place Order'
[DOM] Order confirmation indicates pending/offline payment status — not shown as 'Paid'
[ADMIN] Order in admin with payment status 'Pending' or 'New' (not 'Paid')
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] createOrderFromCart GraphQL mutation errors[] is empty for manual payment flow
[ADMIN] Order payment method recorded as Purchase Order / Wire Transfer in admin order details
Failure_Signals: Manual payment option not shown to B2B user; form incorrectly shows card input fields for manual payment; order placed but shows 'Paid' status immediately (BL-PAY-003 violation); order not visible in admin after placement
References: Checklist #9 item 11; BL-PAY-003; Moved from 040 to 041 (cross-cutting: provider-agnostic manual/PO flow, no card processor involved)

Title: After Decline User Can Switch Payment Method Entirely
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
User logged in; at checkout; multiple payment methods available; first attempt declined
Steps:
[NAV] complete checkout to payment step
[ACT] select first payment method (e.g., CyberSource)
[ACT] enter declined card details and attempt payment
[WAIT] wait for decline error
[ASSERT] verify decline error shown
[ACT] select a different payment method (e.g., Authorize.Net or manual payment)
[ASSERT] verify payment form changes to the new method
[ASSERT] verify cart and shipping still intact after method switch
[ACT] complete payment with the alternative method
[ASSERT] verify order confirmation reached
Assertions:
[DOM] payment method selector still functional after decline
[DOM] new payment form rendered for alternative method
[STATE] checkout state preserved during method switch
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] new payment method used in final order creation
[CONSOLE] no JS errors during method switch
[NETWORK] no 5xx
Failure_Signals: payment method selector locked after decline; cannot switch methods; previous method form persists; checkout state lost during switch
References: Coverage Gap Analysis; Moved from 040 to 041 (cross-cutting: decline→method-switch covers any processor combination, not provider-specific)
